name: Phenytoin
indications:
## Main indications

Anticonvulsant.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.dose: Loading 15–20 mg/kg IV (max. 1500 mg) at ≤ 50 mg/min.

pediatrico.presentation: Phenytoin sodium: Capsules: 100 mg; Suspension: 25 mg/mL; 2 mL ampoules: 50 mg/mL; Phenytoin calcium: Tablets: 100 mg
pediatrico.dose: IV loading dose: 15–20 mg/kg, maximum dose: 1500 mg. IV – PO maintenance: 5–8 mg/kg/day every 12 h. Adults: 300 mg/day every 8–12 h
pediatrico.notes: Monitor CBC, Ca, P, alkaline phosphatase every 6 months. PO: administer away from dairy products. IV push: infusion rate neonates: 0,5 mg/kg/minute; children and adults: 1–3 mg/kg/minute (maximum: 50 mg/min). Intermittent infusion (not recommended): dilute in 0.9% NaCl at concentration < 6 mg/mL. Separate administration by 2 h from antacids and enteral nutrition. Numerous drug interactions (metabolism induction or inhibition). Isoniazid increases risk of phenytoin toxicity: ataxia, hyperreflexia, nystagmus, tremor. Adjust phenytoin dose with serum level. See preliminary guide for prevention of medication-related teratogenesis.

neonatal.dose: 15–20 mg/kg loading; maintenance per NICU levels.

stability:
## General

- Precipitates with 5% dextrose; use 0.9% NaCl.

## Pediatric guide

- Administer within 4 h after dilution.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

Hirsutism, gingival hyperplasia, ataxia, nystagmus, nausea, vomiting, hematologic abnormalities, calcium metabolism disturbance. With rapid administration: arrhythmias.
